title: Fury defeats Wach in seventh round; Joshua bout confirmed for November
dek: Tyson Fury defeated Mariusz Wach by seventh-round stoppage in Pattaya, Thailand on Friday. Wach's corner pulled him from the bout after round seven. Fury and Anthony Joshua have agreed to an all-British heavyweight unification bout in November, backed by promoter Turki Alalshikh, per ESPN.
author: The GoCheckMySports Desk
key_fact: Tyson Fury stopped Mariusz Wach in the seventh round in Pattaya, Thailand on Friday, clearing the path to a November heavyweight unification fight with Anthony Joshua.
bottom_line: Fury's stoppage victory in Thailand moves the Fury-Joshua unification bout closer to reality, contingent on Joshua's Saturday performance against Prenga and both camps' adherence to the November timeline. Monitor Joshua's bout result for any sign of injury or change in readiness.
body: Tyson Fury stopped Mariusz Wach by seventh-round stoppage in Pattaya, Thailand on Friday, per ESPN. Wach's corner pulled their fighter from the bout on his stool after round seven, ending the bout without a knockout.
Wach, 46 years old with a record of 39-14 and 20 knockouts, entered the fight as a tune-up opponent. Fury improved to 36-2-1 with 25 knockouts, according to ESPN's reporting.

The bout drew approximately 1,500 spectators and was not televised. Fury fought second on the card rather than headlining as the final bout of the night, per ESPN. Proceeds from ticket sales were raised for the Father Ray Foundation, a charity in Thailand that supports underprivileged children.

Fury and Anthony Joshua have both agreed to an all-British heavyweight unification bout scheduled for November, according to ESPN. The deal was signed with boxing powerbroker Turki Alalshikh, who is bankrolling the clash. Joshua fights Kristian Prenga in Saudi Arabia on Saturday night, per the reporting, as a final tune-up before the November unification bout.
